title: t-string Template iterator freed with uninitialized fields under OOM (SIGSEGV) — Objects/templateobject.c template_iter
description: `template_iter` (Objects/templateobject.c) allocates the iterator with PyObject_GC_New (no zero-init) and only sets `stringsiter`/`interpolationsiter` after two PyObject_GetIter calls; either GetIter failing under OOM runs Py_DECREF(iter) on the error path, so templateiter_dealloc -> templateiter_clear -> Py_CLEAR() dereferences uninitialized memory.
notes: Uninitialized-field read (PyObject_GC_New does not zero the struct body). Deterministic SIGSEGV on ASan builds (ft_debug_asan, jit) where fresh memory is 0xbe-poisoned; release builds (ft_release, upstream) exit cleanly in the swept budget because a first allocation often reads back zero (field NULL -> Py_CLEAR(NULL) no-op), but a templateiterobject re-allocated from a dirty freelist slot is a latent use-after-free on release too. Both GetIter error paths share the defect; templateiter_traverse has the same exposure if GC runs pre-init. PEP 750 t-string code (new in 3.14). Minimal 3-line repro (t-string literal + set_nomemory sweep). Fix: NULL the two fields immediately after PyObject_GC_New. Found via segv site-sweep (scripts/segv_sweep.sh): the only NEW bug among 254 segv vehicles; the rest deduped to OOM-0001/0002/0003/0004/0005/0006/0008/0013/0020/0022 or were host-only NOREPRO.
